extra_model_fields.expected_outcome: Verifier can parse all artifacts, confirm workbook calculations via CSV/formula dump and acceptance checks, and ri_4 will be marked passed; model and deterministic precheck scores will be reconciled or discrepancy explained.

2) Calculations.csv rows for baseline and each remediation scenario match memo numbers
3) Calculations_formula_dump.txt lists formulas claimed in the memo with cell addresses
4) Memo contains provenance table and sample acceptance-check run
5) Grader metadata has consistent passed_criteria/failed_criteria and rubric_item_scores

planned_changes: Include output_schema.json and acceptance_checks.json artifacts in the attempt deliverables and add a 50-100 line plain-text preview for each in the memo; ensure files are syntactically valid JSON and match the thresholds documented in the memo (fail<0.08, warn 0.08-0.12, pass>=0.12) and budget cutoff (108000).

Add Calculations.csv (computed margins and per-option scenario outputs for R1-R3) and Calculations_formula_dump.txt (cell addresses and exact formula strings) as artifacts; if possible include a small script (reproduce_checks.py) that ingests the CSV and outputs the acceptance_checks.json result so verification can run without openpyxl.
Update the memo with a short provenance table mapping workbook cell addresses to embedded packet fields and a statement that there are no hidden sheets or external inputs.
Run the acceptance_checks script locally and include a sample run (command line and resulting JSON) in the memo to demonstrate machine-checkable verification succeeds.

Fix the grader/reporting inconsistency by ensuring the produced metadata uses a single authoritative pass/fail list and that rubric_item_scores.passed flags match. Add an explicit 'score_discount_explanation' field documenting why the model grader score differs from the deterministic precheck (if applicable).
Re-run the attempt and provide visible previews for the added JSON and CSV artifacts so the verifier can parse them and confirm ri_4 passes.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The baseline analysis is correct and well-documented: numeric recomputations from the memo match hand calculations and remediation trade-offs are reasonable. Primary issues preventing full verification are (1) missing machine-checkable JSON artifacts (output_schema.json and acceptance_checks.json) from the accessible previews, (2) inability to programmatically parse the .xlsx in this environment (openpyxl unavailable) so in-workbook formulas/hidden sheets cannot be confirmed, and (3) internal consistency errors in the grader output where ri_4 is reported as both failed and passed. These issues reduce automated confidence and block a fully-verified acceptance pass.
